purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This file defines the data model and business metrics for order items, enabling analysis of revenue breakdown by food vs drink items, cost calculations, and profit metrics. It serves as a semantic layer for order item analytics.

evidence.description: Semantic model defining order item entities, dimensions, and measures including revenue calculations
Metrics definitions for revenue, food/drink breakdowns, ratios, and derived profit calculations

YAML key unit_tests

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: Provides a standardized way to convert monetary values from cents to dollars across different database platforms for consistent financial reporting.

evidence.description: Macro definition with adapter-specific implementations for converting cents to dollars: default__cents_to_dollars, postgres__cents_to_dollars, bigquery__cents_to_dollars, fabric__cents_to_dollars

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This mart model aggregates order data with item-level details to create a comprehensive view of each order, including cost summaries, item counts, and customer ordering sequence. It enhances raw order data with business-relevant computed fields like whether an order contains food or drink items and the customer's order history rank.

evidence.description: CTE 'order_items_summary' computes aggregated metrics such as supply cost, product price subtotal, and categorized item counts (food/drink) per order
CTE 'compute_booleans' joins order data with summary stats and derives boolean flags for food and drink orders
CTE 'customer_order_count' assigns sequential numbering to orders by customer, indicating order history position
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_orders, model.order_items and writes model.orders
Import reference to order_items
Import reference to orders
Import reference to order_items_summary
dbt reference stg_orders

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business intelligence reporting on supply and product combinations, including supply characteristics like cost and perishability.

evidence.description: Description states 'Supplies dimension table. The grain of the table is one row per supply and product combination.'
Dimensions include supply_name, supply_cost, and is_perishable_supply which represent core business concepts
YAML key semantic_models

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: Maintains cleaned and validated order data with financial integrity checks, providing a reliable foundation for order-based business analysis and reporting.

evidence.description: Description mentions 'Order data with basic cleaning and transformation applied'
Data test validates financial calculation: 'order_total - tax_paid = subtotal'
YAML key models

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: Manages database schema organization by defining rules for where different types of data assets (seeds, models) should be deployed based on environment and naming conventions.

evidence.description: Macro with conditional logic for schema naming based on resource type (seed vs model) and target environment (prod vs non-prod)

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This file defines the 'orders' data mart, which provides a comprehensive view of order data including financial details, item types, and customer information. It serves as a centralized source for order-related analytics and reporting.

evidence.description: Description states 'Order overview data mart, offering key details for each order'

domains.description: Core business domain focused on order processing, revenue calculation, and financial analysis of customer purchases including food/drink breakdowns, cost calculations, and profit metrics.
domains.reasoning: These files share key concepts of order processing, revenue calculation, financial integrity, and item categorization. The marts (order_items.yml/orders.sql) explicitly reference revenue breakdowns, cost calculations, and business metrics, while staging models (stg_orders.yml/stg_order_items.yml) provide cleaned order data with financial checks. The cents_to_dollars macro supports consistent financial reporting across these models.

domains.description: Domain focused on customer behavior analysis, lifetime value calculation, segmentation, and retention metrics derived from order history and spending patterns.
domains.reasoning: These files form a cohesive customer analytics pipeline with shared concepts of customer analytics, lifetime value, order metrics, and segmentation. The marts (customers.yml/customers.sql) aggregate lifetime spending and classify customers, while staging models (stg_customers.yml/stg_customers.sql) provide cleaned customer data for this analysis.

domains.description: Domain managing product catalog data, supply chain information, inventory relationships, and product categorization for operational reporting and analysis.
domains.reasoning: These files share concepts of product catalog, supply chain, inventory management, and product categorization. The marts (supplies.yml/products.yml) define semantic models for business intelligence on supplies and products, while staging models (stg_products.sql/stg_supplies.sql) transform raw product and supply data for downstream analytics.

domains.description: Domain focused on location dimension modeling, store data management, and tax rate analysis for location-based business intelligence.
domains.reasoning: These files form a location analytics pipeline with shared concepts of location dimension, tax analysis, and store data. The mart (locations.yml) establishes location dimension for semantic modeling with tax rate measures, while staging models (stg_locations.sql/stg_locations.yml) provide cleaned location data.

domains.description: Cross-cutting domain managing data pipeline orchestration, schema management, source definitions, and project configuration.
domains.reasoning: These configuration and infrastructure files share concepts of workflow orchestration, data governance, schema management, and project configuration. Taskfile.yml orchestrates data workflows, dbt_project.yml defines project structure, __sources.yml maps data sources, generate_schema_name.sql manages schema organization, and pre-commit config enforces code quality.

domains.description: Supporting domain providing time-series analysis infrastructure and date dimension for metric reporting across business domains.
domains.reasoning: This standalone file creates a comprehensive date spine for time-series analysis and metric reporting, serving as foundational infrastructure for temporal analysis across other business domains.

documentation_drift.explanation: Documentation claims to categorize products as food or drink items, but implementation categorizes based on 'jaffle' and 'beverage' type values, not generic food/drink categories. Documentation also mentions converting price units but doesn't specify the macro function used.

evidence.description: Documentation says 'categorize products as food or drink items' but code uses specific type values 'jaffle' and 'beverage' which may not represent all food/drink items
Documentation mentions 'converts price units' but doesn't reference the specific macro function 'cents_to_dollars' used in implementation

documentation_drift.explanation: Documentation references BigQuery-specific DATE function syntax that doesn't match the actual implementation using dbt_date.get_base_dates macro

evidence.description: Comment suggests using BigQuery DATE function with specific format, but code uses dbt_date.get_base_dates macro instead

documentation_drift.explanation: The inline documentation comments accurately describe the corresponding code sections. Each comment directly precedes and correctly explains the YAML structure it documents.

evidence.description: Comment '#The name of the semantic model.' correctly describes the 'name: products' field on line 3
Comment '#The name of the dbt model and schema' correctly describes the 'model: ref('products')' field on line 7
Comment '#Entities. These usually corespond to keys in the table.' correctly describes the entities section starting on line 9
Comment '#Dimensions. Either categorical or time...' correctly describes the dimensions section starting on line 14

documentation_drift.explanation: Documentation sections ('ids', 'numerics', 'timestamps') don't match actual implementation details. Documentation lacks description of cent-to-dollar conversions and timestamp truncation that are central to the model's purpose.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Defines the product dimension for semantic modeling, providing standardized product attributes and categorizations to support consistent product-based metrics and analysis across the business.

supporting_evidence.description: Description states 'Product dimension table. The grain of the table is one row per product.'
Defines product dimensions including product_name, product_type, product_description, is_food_item, is_drink_item, and product_price

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized view of product data for business reporting and analysis. Enables downstream consumers to access consistent product information without needing to understand the underlying raw data structure.

supporting_evidence.description: Selects all fields from stg_products without transformation, indicating purpose is data presentation rather than business logic
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_products and writes model.products
Import reference to products

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Delivers a unified view of supply chain data for operational reporting and inventory management decisions. Supports business users in understanding supply availability and relationships to products.

supporting_evidence.description: Direct pass-through query selecting all fields from stg_supplies without business transformations
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_supplies and writes model.supplies
Import reference to supplies

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Provides a simplified view of location data by selecting all records from the staging locations model without additional transformation.

supporting_evidence.description: SQL shows direct selection from stg_locations with no additional business logic: 'select * from {{ ref('stg_locations') }}'
Final select statement performs no aggregation or filtering: 'select * from locations'
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_locations and writes model.locations

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that enforce code quality standards and formatting rules before commits are accepted. It automates validation checks for YAML syntax, file formatting, and Python code style.

check-yaml, end-of-file-fixer, trailing-whitespace, requirements-txt-fixer hook definitions
ruff-check and ruff-format hook definitions with fix arguments

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This dbt project configuration file defines the structure and settings for the 'jaffle_shop' analytics project, including model paths, deployment targets, and data transformation rules.
